title: Desire Paths: Footprints of Collective Choice
subtitle: 草地野路揭示规划与真实行为的冲突

paragraphs.en: Navigating beyond the organised pavements and parks of our urban spaces, desire paths are the unofficial footprints of a community, revealing the unspoken preferences, shared shortcuts and collective choices of humans. Often appearing as trodden dirt tracks through otherwise neat green spaces, these routes of collective disobedience cut corners, bisect lawns and cross hills, representing the natural capability of people and animals to go from point A to point B most effectively. Urban planners interpret desire paths as more than just convenient shortcuts; they offer valuable insights into the dynamics between planning and behaviour.
paragraphs.zh: 在规整的人行道与城市公园之外，欲望小径是社区中非官方的足迹，展露了人们心照不宣的偏好、共享的捷径与集体的抉择。这些集体违逆所留下的路线常以踏平的土路形态横贯整洁草坪，它们抄近路、切分绿地、翻越山丘，体现着人与动物最本能的高效通行需求。城市规划者将其视为不仅仅是图方便的捷径，更视之为理解规划与行为之间动态关系的宝贵线索。

paragraphs.en: Ohio State University allowed its students to navigate the Oval, a lawn in the centre of campus, freely, then proceeded to pave the desire paths, creating a web of effective routes students had established. Yet, reluctance persists among other planners to integrate desire paths into formal plans, citing concerns about safety, environmental impact, or primarily, aesthetics.
paragraphs.zh: 俄亥俄州立大学曾允许学生在校园中心的椭圆草坪自由穿行，随后顺势铺筑这些欲望小径，构筑起学生自创的高效路网。然而，其他规划者依然抵触将欲望小径纳入正式规划，理由多涉及安全、环境冲击，或者首要的审美顾虑。

paragraphs.en: A Reddit webpage devoted to the phenomenon, boasting nearly 50,000 members, showcases images of local desire paths adorned with signs instructing pedestrians to adhere to designated walkways, underscoring the rebellious nature inherent in these human-made tracks. This clash highlights an ongoing struggle between the organic, user-driven evolution of public spaces and the desire for a visually curated and controlled urban environment.
paragraphs.zh: 一个聚集近五万会员的Reddit专版展示着各地欲望小径的照片，小径旁赫然立着请走指定步道的标牌，突显了这种人迹小径与生俱来的反叛色彩。此种冲突凸显出一场持续角力：一边是公共空间自发、由使用者驱动的演进，另一边则是追求视觉秩序与可控的都市环境。

paragraphs.en: The Wickquasgeck Trail is an example of a historical desire path, created by Native Americans to cross the forests of Manhattan and move between settlements quickly. This trail, when Dutch colonists arrived, was widened and made into one of the main trade roads across the island, known at the time as de Heere Straat, or Gentlemen's Street. Following the British assumption of control in New York, the street was renamed Broadway.
paragraphs.zh: 威克夸斯盖克步道便是一条历史上的欲望小径，由美洲原住民开辟，用以穿越曼哈顿丛林、在聚落间快速往来。荷兰殖民者到来后，这条小径被拓宽为横贯全岛的主商路，时称赫雷街或绅士街。英国接管纽约后，该街更名为百老汇。

paragraphs.en: Notably, Broadway stands out as one of the few areas in New York City that defies the grid-based system applied to the rest of the city, cutting a diagonal across parts of the city. In online spaces, desire paths have sparked a fascination that can approach obsession, with the Reddit page serving as a hub. Contributors offer a wide array of stories, from little-known new shortcuts to long-established alternate routes.
paragraphs.zh: 值得注意的是，百老汇是纽约市极少数打破网格布局的地区之一，斜向切割着部分城区。在网络空间里，欲望小径引发近乎痴迷的追捧，Reddit专版正是焦点所在。投稿者贡献的故事包罗万象，从鲜为人知的新捷径到历久经年的老便道，不一而足。

paragraphs.en: Animal desire paths, such as ducks forging trails through frozen ponds or dogs carving direct routes in gardens, highlight the adaptability of these trails in both human and animal experiences. As desire paths criss-cross through both physical and virtual landscapes, they stand as a proof of the collective insistence on forging unconventional routes and embracing the spirit of communal choice.
paragraphs.zh: 动物界的欲望小径，比如鸭群踏冰穿塘、犬只园中直行，都彰显了此类路径在人与动物经验中的共通适应性。欲望小径交错于实体与虚拟地貌之间，它们是集体执意另辟蹊径、拥抱公共选择精神的有力证明。
